_comment: TASK-053 / ADR-006 §5.4 — committed M3 baseline for the near-Sol tier-unification budget gate. Recorded by running the SAME probe in baseline mode against the M3 tier (HYG-only octree + always-on HYG monolith + procgen) on the identical committed flythrough3 path. The M4a run (combined HYG+Gaia octree, gated monolith, coverage-faded procgen) must come in AT OR BELOW these near-Sol numbers despite Gaia adding stars to the field — proving fewer redundant layers (ADR-006 §5.4 rule 4).
_howToRecord: Start the preview server (pnpm --filter @cosmos/web preview --port 4173), open http://localhost:4173/?debug=flythrough4&baseline=m3 in Chromium with --use-angle=swiftshader, wait for window.__flythrough4Result, then copy result.segments.toSol.{peakRenderedPoints,peakDrawCalls} and result.segments.toEarth.{...} into nearSol below (take the max of toSol+toEarth for each metric — that is the inner 'near Sol' segment). e2e/tests/flythrough4.spec.ts records this automatically when run with UPDATE_BASELINE=1.
_recordedNote: Recorded 2026-06-22 on chromium+SwiftShader via ?debug=flythrough4&baseline=m3. CAVEAT (NEW finding, see TASK-053 report): on this scripted descent the octree contributes ZERO rendered points and catalogCoverage stays 0 in BOTH the M3 and M4a tiers — octree tiles arrive out-of-cut and release the same frame, so the 1,000,000 points / 1 draw call below is the PROCGEN Milky Way cloud, not catalog tiles. The near-Sol M3↔M4a budget comparison is therefore degenerate (equal, both procgen-only) until the octree-never-covers-the-cut defect is fixed. Re-record after that fix lands.

nearSol._comment: max over the toSol + toEarth segments (the inner, near-Sol path).

caps._comment: Inherited §5.8 hard caps — these gate regardless of the relative comparison and never relax (TASK-053 forbidden actions).
